topic: Zep AI Review 2026: Temporal Knowledge Graphs for Agent Memory

key_points: Mem0 is the most widely adopted agent memory framework with ~48K GitHub stars
Mem0 scores 49.0% on LongMemEval vs Zep's 63.8% — 15-point gap driven by Zep's temporal graph
Mem0 offers up to 80% prompt token reduction through intelligent chat history compression
Graph features (Mem0g) only available on Pro tier at $249/mo
Mem0 is the safer, well-rounded choice with broader integrations
LangMem is the developer's choice for LangGraph shops — free, open source, self-managed
LangMem stores memories as flat key-value items with vector search only — no entity extraction or relationship modeling
LangMem requires managing your own infrastructure
Best for: teams already using LangChain/LangGraph ecosystem
Letta (formerly MemGPT) models agent memory like an OS: main context as RAM, external storage as disk
Letta is the only choice for agents that need to operate independently for days
Cognee implements GraphRAG to transform raw text into structured Knowledge Graphs for multi-document environments
EverMind AI (EverOS) claims highest benchmarks: LoCoMo 93.05%, LongMemEval-S 83.00% — outperforming Zep's 63.8%
8 major agent memory frameworks now compete in the 2026 market
Zep's credit-based pricing model can be confusing for teams estimating costs
Self-hosting Graphiti requires provisioning a graph database (Neo4j, FalkorDB, or Kuzu)
Zep Community Edition was deprecated in April 2025 — no complete self-hosted option remains
Steep learning curve compared to simpler alternatives like Mem0
Cognee focuses on complex multi-document GraphRAG vs Zep's conversational temporal memory
Zep excels at tracking how facts and relationships change over time in agent conversations
Cognee better suited for knowledge base Q&A, Zep better for long-running agent tasks

stats.fact: Zep achieves 94.8% accuracy on the DMR (Deep Memory Retrieval) benchmark vs MemGPT's 93.4%
Zep scores 98.2% on DMR with GPT-4o Mini, setting new state-of-the-art
On LongMemEval, Zep scores 63.8% vs Mem0's 49.0% — a 15-point gap driven by temporal knowledge graph
Zep delivers accuracy improvements up to 18.5% while reducing response latency by 90% vs baseline full-context
Zep shows 184% improvement over full-context retrieval for Preference-Based Questions
Graphiti crossed 20,000 GitHub stars in 2026 with 25,000 weekly PyPI downloads and 35+ contributors
Zep Cloud delivers context retrieval with <200ms latency at enterprise scale
Zep Free plan includes 1,000 credits/month; 1 credit = one Episode up to 350 bytes
Zep Community Edition was deprecated April 2025; self-hosting now requires raw Graphiti + graph database
Zep shows 38.4% improvement on temporal reasoning tasks vs full-context approach

angles: Temporal knowledge graphs are fundamentally different from vector stores — facts have validity windows (valid_at/invalid_at), not just timestamps
Why Zep beats RAG for agent memory: relationship-aware retrieval vs semantic similarity alone
The 90% latency reduction story: how Zep's structured memory outperforms stuffing full chat history into context
Zep's three-tier architecture explained: episodes → entities → communities (hierarchical context building)
The deprecation of Zep Community Edition: what it means for open-source self-hosters in 2026
When to choose Zep over Mem0: temporal reasoning vs personalization trade-offs
Enterprise angle: SOC2 Type 2 + HIPAA compliance makes Zep viable for healthcare and fintech AI agents
Graphiti as standalone library: using Zep's open-source core without the cloud platform
